message:
feat(deps): switch frankensqlite and franken-agent-detection to local path dependencies and harden FTS fallback resilience

Switches the two pinned git revision dependencies (frankensqlite and
franken-agent-detection) to local path references, eliminating version
drift between the local workspace and remote commits. This unifies the
dependency graph so cargo resolves a single copy of each crate instead of
maintaining separate git-rev and path versions.

Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock:
- Replace fsqlite git+rev=c0d2c375 with path = "../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite"
  and fsqlite-types similarly
- Replace franken-agent-detection git+rev=ba9c598 with path = "../franken_agent_detection"
- Cargo.lock reflects the unified resolution: one entry per crate instead of
  git+path duplicates; windows-sys versions normalize downward accordingly
- Bump build.rs frankensqlite rev marker for cache invalidation

Indexer robustness (src/indexer/mod.rs):
- Add FTS fallback repair as an explicit post-index maintenance step via
  ensure_search_fallback_fts_consistency() — runs only after successful
  indexing rather than on every database open
- Track and log FTS repair outcomes (AlreadyHealthy, IncrementalCatchUp,
  Rebuilt) with span-level tracing for observability
- Add graceful degradation: when FTS repair fails, log a warning and
  continue rather than aborting the index run, since Tantivy is authoritative
- Extend E2E test harness with helper functions for FTS schema verification
  and dedicated repair-after-index assertions

Search query resilience (src/search/query.rs):
- Add explicit disambiguation between read-only sqlite_guard() opens (which
  must never trigger FTS repair) and writable indexer opens
- Clean up test naming and assertions to reflect the read-only-open contract

Storage layer (src/storage/sqlite.rs):
- Expose ensure_search_fallback_fts_consistency() unconditionally (remove
  #[cfg(test)] gate) so the indexer maintenance path can call it
- Similarly ungating read_fts_franken_rebuild_generation(),
  record_fts_franken_rebuild_generation(), ensure_fts_consistency_via_
  frankensqlite(), and the FTS constants they depend on

E2E test expansion (tests/e2e_search_index.rs):
- Replace shell-exec sqlite3/python3 fixture helpers with direct rusqlite
  calls for schema corruption injection — more reliable, no external process
  dependency
- Add comprehensive FTS-repair-via-index test flow: inject duplicate schema
  rows, verify search still works via Tantivy, run index, verify FTS is
  repaired to exactly one schema row with all content preserved
- Add post-repair incremental indexing test: write new content after repair,
  verify it's searchable
- Add health-check assertions after repair confirming the database reports
  healthy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+    #[test]
+    fn tantivy_reader_skips_sqlite_fallback_on_empty_lexical_results() -> Result<()> {
+        let dir = TempDir::new()?;
+        let mut index = TantivyIndex::open_or_create(dir.path())?;
+        index.commit()?;
+        let reader = fs_cass_open_search_reader(dir.path(), ReloadPolicy::Manual).ok();
+        assert!(
+            reader.is_some(),
+            "test fixture should open a Tantivy reader even with an empty index"
+        );
+
+        let conn = Connection::open(":memory:")?;
+        conn.execute_batch(
+            "CREATE TABLE sources (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, kind TEXT);
+             CREATE TABLE agents (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE);
+             CREATE TABLE workspaces (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE);
+             CREATE TABLE conversations (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                agent_id INTEGER,
+                workspace_id INTEGER,
+                source_id TEXT,
+                origin_host TEXT,
+                title TEXT,
+                source_path TEXT
+             );
+             CREATE TABLE messages (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                conversation_id INTEGER,
+                idx INTEGER,
+                content TEXT,
+                created_at INTEGER
+             );
+             CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE fts_messages USING fts5(
+                content,
+                title,
+                agent,
+                workspace,
+                source_path,
+                created_at UNINDEXED,
+                content='',
+                tokenize='porter'
+             );",
+        )?;
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO sources(id, kind) VALUES('local', 'local')")?;
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO agents(id, slug) VALUES(1, 'codex')")?;
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO workspaces(id, path) VALUES(1, '/sqlite-only')")?;
+        conn.execute(
+            "INSERT INTO conversations(id, agent_id, workspace_id, source_id, origin_host, title, source_path)
+             VALUES(1, 1, 1, 'local', NULL, 'sqlite fallback only', '/tmp/sqlite-only.jsonl')",
+        )?;
+        conn.execute(
+            "INSERT INTO messages(id, conversation_id, idx, content, created_at)
+             VALUES(1, 1, 0, 'sqliteonlytoken overflow candidate', 42)",
+        )?;
+        conn.execute_compat(
+            "INSERT INTO fts_messages(rowid, content, title, agent, workspace, source_path, created_at)
+             VALUES(?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)",
+            params![
+                1_i64,
+                "sqliteonlytoken overflow candidate",
+                "sqlite fallback only",
+                "codex",
+                "/sqlite-only",
+                "/tmp/sqlite-only.jsonl",
+                42_i64
+            ],
+        )?;
+
+        let client = SearchClient {
+            reader,
+            sqlite: Mutex::new(Some(SendConnection(conn))),
+            sqlite_path: None,
+            prefix_cache: Mutex::new(CacheShards::new(*CACHE_TOTAL_CAP, *CACHE_BYTE_CAP)),
+            reload_on_search: true,
+            last_reload: Mutex::new(None),
+            last_generation: Mutex::new(None),
+            reload_epoch: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),
+            warm_tx: None,
+            _warm_handle: None,
+            metrics: Metrics::default(),
+            cache_namespace: format!("v{CACHE_KEY_VERSION}|schema:test"),
+            semantic: Mutex::new(None),
+            last_tantivy_total_count: Mutex::new(None),
+        };
+
+        let sqlite_hits = client.search_sqlite_fts5(
+            Path::new(":memory:"),
+            "sqliteonlytoken",
+            SearchFilters::default(),
+            5,
+            0,
+            FieldMask::FULL,
+        )?;
+        assert_eq!(
+            sqlite_hits.len(),
+            1,
+            "fixture should prove sqlite fallback would have produced a hit"
+        );
+
+        let tantivy_authoritative_hits = client.search(
+            "sqliteonlytoken",
+            SearchFilters::default(),
+            5,
+            0,
+            FieldMask::FULL,
+        )?;
+        assert!(
+            tantivy_authoritative_hits.is_empty(),
+            "a live Tantivy reader should prevent sqlite fallback from populating empty lexical results"
+        );
+        Ok(())
+    }
